_specification: Husqvarna Forest & Garden Web Design Specification
_color_adjustment: All Husqvarna colors have +10% black mixed in per brand spec
_critical_rules: Page backgrounds: ONLY White, Grey 100, or Grey 200
Header/footer: ONLY White or Husqvarna Blue
Functional colors: ONLY for their defined purpose, never decorative
All buy-now/add-to-cart buttons: MUST be Active Green

husqvarna.note: All Husqvarna colors have +10% black mixed in per brand spec

functional.usage-note: Functional colors must only be used for their defined purpose - never decoratively

neutral.note: All neutrals adjusted with +10% black per brand spec

backgrounds.rule: Only these three colors may be used as page backgrounds

cards.rule: Cards must contrast with their background - non-negotiable

text.rule: Grey 500 and 700 must not be used for body text on bright backgrounds

dividers.rule: Only Grey 300 or Grey 400 permitted for dividers

header-footer.rule: Headers and footers must be White or Husqvarna Blue only

hover.rule: Always use black with opacity, never solid black or colored tints

spacing.rule: All spacing must be multiples of 4px base unit

typography._CRITICAL: ONLY Inter font allowed for Husqvarna F&G - no exceptions

fontSize.rule: Start with body text size, derive other styles via modular scale

lineHeight.rule: Approximately 1.5x font size, must be divisible by 4px

letterSpacing.rule: Increase slightly for small text to improve legibility - use sparingly

contrast.rule: 30% reduction from WCAG AA baseline (4.5:1). Avoid both too-low and too-high contrast

lineLength.rule: Lines longer than 100 characters cause reader fatigue

breakpoints.rule: Design must adapt at these exact breakpoints

columns.rule: 12 columns standard, use 4/6/8 on small screens

grid.flexibility: Mix columns freely (e.g., 8+4, 6+4+2) as long as total = 12
grid.alignment: All elements must align to 4px base unit

design-principles.1: Mobile first - design for mobile, then scale up
design-principles.2: Best practice - users expect familiar UX patterns
design-principles.3: Keep it tidy - less is more, focus on essentials
design-principles.4: Design for the user - aesthetics enhance perceived usability
